Home / Alternatives to NordVPN / NordVPN vs WireGuard

NordVPN vs WireGuard

A side-by-side look at NordVPN (the paid SaaS) and WireGuard (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

NordVPN WireGuard
Tagline Consumer VPN subscription with a large server network. Modern VPN tunnel protocol with a tiny code base.
License Proprietary SaaS GPL-2.0
Pricing From $3.99/month on 2-year plans; renewal rates are higher. Free to self-host
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) No
Desktop apps Varies by product Windows, macOS, Linux
Mobile apps Official apps typically available iOS, Android
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Running your own fast VPN tunnel on a $5 VPS with minimal moving parts.

WireGuard strengths

  • Fast, auditable (4k LoC) and shipped in Linux kernel.
  • Trivial config once you understand keys and peers.
  • Runs on anything from a VPS to a Raspberry Pi.

WireGuard weaknesses

  • No built-in user management — you manage keys yourself.
  • No obfuscation — firewalls can detect and block it.
  • You're accountable for the exit IP's reputation.

What's the catch with NordVPN?

  • You still have to trust the provider with your traffic.
  • Apps on some platforms lag behind others on features.
  • Aggressive auto-renew pricing catches people out.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to NordVPN: see NordVPN alternatives, or learn more about WireGuard on its project page.
